|             | Approach  | 4.0            | 4.4               | +10%       |
|             | Idle/Taxi | 26.0           | 14.8              | -43%       |

title: Operational_Aircraft_Emissions
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 6]
Here is the extracted technical content:

1.  **Mathematical Equations**

    `sum of the four LTO mode products of time-in-mode × fuel flow × emission-index`

2.  **Tables**

    Table 2-2: US EPA Default time-in-modes¹

    | Aircraft Category                  | Take-off | Climbout | Approach | Taxi/Idle |
    | :--------------------------------- | :------- | :------- | :------- | :-------- |
    | Commercial carrier:                |          |          |          |           |
    | Jumbo, long and medium range jet | 0.7      | 2.2      | 4.0      | 26.0      |
    | Turboprop                          | 0.5      | 2.5      | 4.5      | 26.0      |
    | Transport - piston                 | 0.6      | 5.0      | 4.6      | 13.0      |
    | General Aviation:                  |          |          |          |           |
    | Business Jet                       | 0.4      | 0.5      | 1.6      | 13.0      |
    | Turboprop                          | 0.5      | 2.5      | 4.5      | 26.0      |
    | Piston                             | 0.3      | 5.0      | 6.0      | 16.0      |
    | Helicopter                         | -        | 6.5      | 6.5      | 7.0       |

    Time in Mode Units: minutes

3.  **Diagrams**

    *   Conventional LTO Emission Calculation
        *   **Engine & LTO Cycle**
            *   ICAO Emission Database
        *   **Aircraft Information**
            *   Tail number
            *   Aircraft type
            *   Engine Type
            *   Number of Engines
        *   **Operational Information(Airport)**
            *   Taxi-time

    LTO Emissions of Air Traffic for different species

**Table 5-1: Differences in time in mode (times in minutes)**

| Mode | ICAO LTO | Operational LTO | Difference |
|---|---|---|---|
| Take-off | 0.7 | 1.6 | +130% |
| Climb-out | 2.2 | 0.5 | -77% |
| Approach | 4.0 | 4.4 | +10% |
| Taxi / Ground Idle | 26.0 | 14.8 | -43% |

**Table 5-2: Differences in fuel consumption (operational LTO to ICAO LTO)**

| Aircraft | Take-off | Climb-out | Approach | Taxi/Idle | LTO Cycle |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Airbus A319 | 107% | -94% | -29% | -58% | -45% |
| Airbus A320/DAC | 73% | -84% | -31% | -60% | -47% |
| Airbus A320/SAC | 73% | -84% | -34% | -54% | -45% |
| Airbus A321 | 73% | -81% | -33% | -54% | -43% |
| Airbus A330/RR | 77% | -68% | -43% | -63% | -44% |
| Airbus A330/PW | 117% | -69% | -46% | -35% | -29% |
| Airbus A340 | 229% | -75% | -34% | -32% | -14% |
| Boeing B757 | 66% | -84% | -31% | -65% | -49% |
| Boeing B767 | 93% | -90% | -28% | -58% | -43% |
| Average | 101% | -81% | -34% | -53% | -38% |

**Table 5-3: Differences in NOx-emissions (operational LTO to ICAO LTO)**

| Aircraft | Take-off | Climb-out | Approach | Taxi / Idle | LTO Cycle |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Airbus A319 | 97% | -94% | -52% | -58% | -41% |
| Airbus A320/DAC | 49% | -86% | -45% | -60% | -44% |

        *   X-axis: Wf - lbm/hr (Fuel Flow in pounds per hour). Values range from 1000 to 20000
    *   **Curves:**
        *   Three segmented linear curves (presumably for EINOx as stated in paragraph 1.3)
        *   Two linear extrapolations (presumably for EIHC and EICO as stated in paragraph 1.4)
    *   **Key Values:**
        *   The curves represent fits between pairs of points [7% to 30%; 30%-85% and 85% to 100%]

title: Operational_Aircraft_Emissions
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 15]
1. Mathematical equations:
EINOX'TO = POTENZ(10;(logEINOXTO - logEINOXCL) / (log ffTO - log ffCL)* (log ff'TO - log ffCL) + logEINOXCL

2. Graphs/charts:
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

3. Tables:
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

4. Diagrams:
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

5. Key numbers, constants, parameters:
- EINOx = emission index NOx (ICAO)
- EINOX' = interpolated emission index NOx
- TO = take-off
- CL = climb
- ID = idle
- ff = fuel flow (ICAO)
- ff' = measured fuel flow (EMS)
